headline: Google DeepMind outlines new evaluation benchmarks for long-context reasoning
summary: The benchmarks probe how models hold up as context windows grow, a question directly relevant to RAG vs. long-context tradeoffs.

headline: OpenAI details cost and latency tradeoffs for its latest model tier
summary: New pricing tiers trade throughput for lower per-token cost, shifting the math on when retrieval is worth adding.

headline: New arXiv survey compares retrieval-augmented generation against long-context prompting
summary: The survey finds RAG still wins on cost and freshness even as context windows grow, but long-context narrows the accuracy gap.

headline: Hugging Face publishes an open benchmark for embedding models used in retrieval
summary: A shared leaderboard for retrieval-quality embeddings, aimed at teams choosing a model for a RAG pipeline.

headline: AWS ML Blog: right-sizing vector search infrastructure to cut inference spend
summary: Guidance on matching index size and instance type to query volume instead of over-provisioning by default.

headline: Report estimates data center electricity demand from AI workloads keeps climbing through 2030
summary: Inference, not training, is flagged as the fastest-growing share of that demand as usage scales.

headline: Anthropic shares guidance on when retrieval beats fine-tuning for enterprise knowledge bases
summary: A practical decision framework: retrieval for fast-changing knowledge, fine-tuning for stable style and format.

headline: Survey: most enterprise AI pilots still stall before reaching production
summary: Grounding and evaluation gaps, more than model quality, are cited as the top blockers past the pilot stage.

headline: New arXiv paper proposes a cheaper hybrid lexical + dense retrieval for large document sets
summary: Combining a lexical first pass with dense re-ranking cuts vector-index size without much of an accuracy hit.

headline: Analysis: falling token prices haven't closed the cost gap between RAG and raw generation
summary: Retrieval itself is nearly free — the added cost comes from the extra context tokens sent to the model.

headline: Hugging Face adds energy-use estimates to its model cards
summary: Per-inference energy figures now sit alongside accuracy numbers, making the tradeoff visible at model-selection time.

headline: Google AI publishes updated responsible-AI guidelines for retrieval-based assistants
summary: New guidance on citing sources and handling conflicting retrieved evidence in assistant-style products.
